summary: Proper variable management in Playwright ensures maintainable, readable, and resilient tests by centralizing selectors, securing configuration, and preventing stale references.
reasoning: User-facing locators are resilient to DOM structure changes and reflect how users interact with the application
Environment variables prevent credential leakage and support multi-environment testing
Playwright tests run in isolated browser contexts by default, making shared mutable state unreliable
const prevents accidental reassignment, reducing bugs in asynchronous test code
Page Object Model centralizes selectors so DOM changes only require updates in one place
